title: ADVANCING WHOLE PERSON DIABETES CARE: FEASIBILITY OF EMBEDDING SOCIAL NEEDS SCREENING INTO DIABETES SELF-MANAGEMENT EDUCATION DELIVERY
authors_full: Dr. Niko Verdecias-Pellum, DrPH, MPH; Assistant Professor; Population Health

summary.hook: This poster presents a qualitative multiple case study evaluating whether it is feasible to integrate health-related social needs (HRSN) screening and referral pathways into...
summary.summary_30s: This poster presents a qualitative multiple case study evaluating whether it is feasible to integrate health-related social needs (HRSN) screening and referral pathways into diabetes self-management education (DSME) delivered in community-based settings.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster presents a qualitative multiple case study evaluating whether it is feasible to integrate health-related social needs (HRSN) screening and referral pathways...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Qualitative multiple case study. 15 DSME facilitators and organizational leaders
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: DSME programs improve health behaviors, but HRSN (e.g., food insecurity, housing instability, transportation) can hinder DSME engagement and outcomes; many DSME programs do not...
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Embedding brief screening at DSME intake/enrollment could operationalize social-needs identification and improve referral planning.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: Which HRSN domains (food, housing, transportation, utilities, safety, etc.) were most commonly raised by facilitators in your interviews?

takeaways: DSME programs improve health behaviors, but HRSN (e.g., food insecurity, housing instability, transportation) can hinder DSME engagement and outcomes; many DSME programs...
A facilitator quote emphasizes that participants may stop attending due to unmet life needs (“…it’s just…interest…survival.”).
Conclusion: Integrating HRSN screening into DSME workflows is feasible and valuable while maintaining program fidelity; training navigators/DSME facilitators supports...

general: What is the main takeaway you hope attendees remember from this poster?

undergrad: What part of this project would be most helpful for an undergraduate student to learn from?

grad: Which HRSN domains (food, housing, transportation, utilities, safety, etc.) were most commonly raised by facilitators in your interviews?

researcher: Which HRSN domains (food, housing, transportation, utilities, safety, etc.) were most commonly raised by facilitators in your interviews?

leader: What type of support, infrastructure, or investment would help this work move further?
